verdict.summary: Round 2 resolves all three prior major findings. The new proposal consistently characterizes the mechanism as issuer self-attestation that scopes and attributes, but does not discharge, the honest-issuer assumption; it expressly denies verifier-checkable same-byte derivation and adversarial-issuer soundness. Historical credentials are excluded by an epoch/marker rule and require re-signing for the signature-covered lane. Value/term identity and leaf collapse are now limited fail-closed to an explicitly enumerated, audited per-datatype injectivity allow-list, with many-to-one datatypes retaining their identity handle. The companion field-native design is updated consistently. This documentation-only, explicitly unaudited proposal makes no uncaveated ZK soundness/privacy claim and introduces no executable security path or vacuous test concern.
